questions.question: Two cubes each of volume $64 \text{ cm}^3$ are joined end to end. Find the surface area of the resulting cuboid.

steps: Determine the side of the cube using the volume formula $a^3 = 64$.
Identify the dimensions of the new cuboid ($l=8, b=4, h=4$) and calculate the surface area using $2(lb + bh + lh)$.
solution.derivation: a = \sqrt[3]{64} = 4 \text{ cm}. \text{ New dimensions: } l=8, b=4, h=4. \text{ SA} = 2(8 \times 4 + 4 \times 4 + 8 \times 4) = 2(32 + 16 + 32) = 2(80) = 160 \text{ cm}^2.

questions.question: A metallic sphere of radius $4.2 \text{ cm}$ is melted and recast into the shape of a cylinder of radius $6 \text{ cm}$. Find the height of the cylinder.

steps: Equate the volume of the sphere to the volume of the cylinder: $\frac{4}{3}\pi r_1^3 = \pi r_2^2 h$.
Solve for $h$ by substituting the given values.

questions.question: The slant height of a frustum of a cone is $4 \text{ cm}$ and the perimeters of its circular ends are $18 \text{ cm}$ and $6 \text{ cm}$. Find the curved surface area of the frustum.

steps: Use the formula for curved surface area of a frustum: $CSA = \pi (r_1 + r_2)l$.
Since $2\pi r_1 = 18$ and $2\pi r_2 = 6$, note that $\pi(r_1 + r_2) = \frac{18+6}{2} = 12$.

questions.question: A solid is in the form of a right circular cone mounted on a hemisphere. If the radius of the hemisphere and the cone is $3.5 \text{ cm}$ and the height of the cone is $4 \text{ cm}$, find the volume of the solid. (Use $\pi = 22/7$)

steps: Volume of solid = Volume of cone + Volume of hemisphere.

questions.question: The diameter of a metallic ball is $4.2 \text{ cm}$. What is the mass of the ball, if the density of the metal is $8.9 \text{ g/cm}^3$?

steps: Calculate the volume of the sphere using $V = \frac{4}{3}\pi r^3$ where $r = 2.1 \text{ cm}$.
